'''Adam Neal''' (born 21 May 1990) is a former English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Neal's position of choice is as a {{rlp|pr}}.

==Background==
Neal was born in [[Irlam]], near [[City of Salford|Salford]], England.

==Career==
He previously played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]] and [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. 
He made his début for [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Challenge Cup]] victory against [[Leigh Centurions]] on 20 April 2008.<ref name="Wolves made to work by Leigh">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/newsview.ink?matchid=11457&group=main&type=m&storytype=RL |title=Wolves made to work by Leigh |publisher=Warrington Wolves |date=2008-04-20 |accessdate=2008-04-21 }}{{dead link|date=June 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/warrington/7359030.stm |title=Cullen praises Warrington rookies |work=BBC Sport |date=21 April 2008 |access-date=20 November 2023}}</ref>

==Coaching==
After retiring in 2018 it was reported that he had joined the [[Oldham RLFC]] backroom strength and conditioning staff<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=21 Nov 2018|accessdate=29 Jun 2021|title=Retired forward Adam Neal joins Oldham's backroom staff
|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/retired-forward-adam-neal-joins-oldhams-backroom-staff/}}</ref>
